# Post-Run Drift Audit

Use this prompt immediately after any documentation maintenance, metadata migration, or
significant code-change agent run. It invokes the read-only auditor and returns a
standardised report that determines whether the repository is in a shippable documentation
state.

## Instructions

### Step 1 — Identify the run context

Read the most recent entry in each of the following to establish what changed:

- [PROJECT_STATUS.md](../../PROJECT_STATUS.md)
- [CHANGELOG.md](../../CHANGELOG.md)
- [MIGRATION_PLAN.md](../../MIGRATION_PLAN.md)

Search for recently modified markdown files to scope the audit to the affected area.

### Step 2 — Invoke the auditor

Apply all checks defined in
[documentation-auditor-readonly.agent.md](../agents/documentation-auditor-readonly.agent.md).

Scope in this order:

1. Repository root markdown files.
2. Active `docs/` directory (exclude `docs/archive/` by default).
3. `.github/` directory (agents, instructions, prompts, templates, workflow doc-comments).
4. `CHANGELOG.md` accuracy against discoverable recent commits.

Only include archive trees when explicitly requested.

Archive hygiene rules for this report:

- Deprecated docs still present in active locations are **High** severity.
- Canonical docs linking stale pre-archive active paths are **High** severity.
- Recommend archive move plus active-link updates when these issues are found.

Do not edit any file during this step.

### Step 3 — Compile the report

Produce the full report using the format below.

## Report Format

```
## Post-Run Drift Audit Report

### Metadata
- Prompt: post-run-drift-audit
- Timestamp: <ISO-8601 datetime>
- Triggered by: <description of the preceding agent run or manual invocation>
- Scope: <comma-separated list of top-level paths audited>
- Files scanned: <count>
- Findings: <total> (<C> Critical, <H> High, <M> Medium, <L> Low)

### Executive Summary
<2–4 sentences: overall documentation health, most significant risks, whether
the repository is ready to ship or requires remediation before release.>

### Findings by Severity

#### Critical
| ID | File | Description | Remediation |
|---|---|---|---|
| CRIT-01 | … | … | … |

#### High
| ID | File | Description | Remediation |
|---|---|---|---|
| HIGH-01 | … | … | … |

#### Medium
| ID | File | Description | Remediation |
|---|---|---|---|
| MED-01 | … | … | … |

#### Low
| ID | File | Description | Remediation |
|---|---|---|---|
| LOW-01 | … | … | … |

### Drift Hotspots
Files with the most outstanding issues, ranked by finding count:

| Rank | File | Finding Count | Highest Severity |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | … | … | … |

### Documentation Update Queue
Ordered by severity then estimated effort (small / medium / large).
Hand off to [documentation-maintainer](../agents/documentation-maintainer.agent.md)
for execution.

1. [Critical] <action> — <file> — <effort>
2. [High] <action> — <file> — <effort>
…

### Gate Decision

**<Pass | Pass with Follow-ups | Blocked>**

Criteria applied:
- Pass: zero Critical and High findings.
- Pass with Follow-ups: zero Critical findings; one or more High findings with
  documented remediation steps.
- Blocked: one or more Critical findings.

Rationale: <1–2 sentences explaining the decision.>

### Residual Uncertainty
- <area the audit could not verify, e.g. inaccessible external links>
- <assumptions made due to missing context>
```

## When to Use This Prompt

- After every run of [documentation-maintainer.agent.md](../agents/documentation-maintainer.agent.md).
- Before cutting a release tag (use alongside [release-docs.prompt.md](release-docs.prompt.md)).
- After a significant merge to `develop` or `main` that touches documentation.
- On demand when documentation health is uncertain.

## Automated Invocation

The `.claude/settings.json` hooks trigger
[scripts/ops/run_post_agent_docs_audit.py](../../scripts/ops/run_post_agent_docs_audit.py)
on `Stop` and `SubagentStop` events. That script performs a deterministic subset of
the checks defined here and writes its output to
`artifacts/drift_reports/latest_post_run_drift_report.md`.

This prompt produces the full AI-assisted audit; the script produces the deterministic
structural audit. Use both together for complete coverage.

## Style Constraints

All content generated by this prompt must follow
[docs-style.instructions.md](../instructions/docs-style.instructions.md).

## References

1. [documentation-auditor-readonly.agent.md](../agents/documentation-auditor-readonly.agent.md) —
   defines audit checks applied by this prompt.
2. [docs-style.instructions.md](../instructions/docs-style.instructions.md) —
   style rules used as baseline for structural checks.
3. [scripts/ops/run_post_agent_docs_audit.py](../../scripts/ops/run_post_agent_docs_audit.py) —
   deterministic counterpart to this prompt.
